content.full_text:
Re:
Fnrn Pollrrcel Pnncrrce s C oMMrssroN
.128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 95gl.l-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
May 7,2009
Ted A. Gaebler
City Manager
City of Rancho Cordova
2729 Prospect Park Drive
Rancho Cordova, California 95876
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-09-097
QUESTTON
May you accept payments for speaking engagements as part of your speaking
engagement business now that you have become the City Manager of the City of Rancho
Cordova?
CONCLUSION
You may not accept payments for speaking engagements without violating the
Act's prohibition on receiving honoraria. The "*""ption for eamed income for personal
services which are customarily provided in connection with a bona fide business, trade or
profession does not apply to you because the sole or predominant activity of your
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Governmenr Code Sections g 1000 through g l0l4. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of ritle 2 of the california
Code of Regulations. All regulatory refbrences are to Title 2, Divisioi 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
Dear Mr. Gaebler:
This letter is_in response to your request for advice regarding the honorarium
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note, the Commission will not
advise with respecr to pasr conducr. (Regulation 1g329(b)(g)(A), copy enclosed.)
Therefore, nothing in this letter should be construed to evaluate any conduct that may
have already taken place, and any conclusions contained in this letier apply o"fy ro
prospective actions.


File No. A-09-097
Page No. 2
business, trade, or profession is making speeches
FACTS
You have been,the City Manager for the City of Rancho Cordova since october
2003' From 1986 to 2ool, you o*n"d a company *hor" primary business consisted of
providing speaking engagements around the worid. Because you authored a book that
became an international best seller, you continue to receive invitations to speak to groups
of business associations' goverlment officials and employees both within and outside of
califomia. Your written employment agreement with-thJ city of Rancho cordova
permits you to engage in "independent acts or other forms of enterpris" au.irg tt or"
hours not covered by active City employment, providing such acts do not constitute a
conflict of interest as defined herein.i' ihe agri"ment firther provides ,t ui you *,
subject to the conflict-of-interest provisions or *r. California Govemment Code and that
you must submit to the city clerk the "appropriate conflict of Interests statements at the
time of appointment, annually thereafter, and at the time of separation from the position.,,
You wish to continue receiving payment for speeches based on your understanding that
your employment agreement does not prohibii this activity and ihat, because you -" u
professional speaker, these payments are income and not prohibited honoraria.
ANALYSIS
section 89502(a) provides that "no elected state officer, elected officer of a local
government agency, or other individual specified in Section B72oO shall accept any
honorarium." Because city manager is a position listed in Section gl2O1t,yl,i,,'uy rro,
accept any honorarium.
section 89501 defines the term "honorarium,,, in pertinent part, as follows:
"(a) For purposes of this chapter, .honorarium, means,
except as provided in subdivision (b), any payment made in
consideration for any speech given, articie pubtished, or
attendance at any public or private conference, convention,
meeting, social event, meal, or like gathering.
"(b) The commission shail adopt regulations to implement
this subdivision.,'
Section 89501(bxl) exempts from the definition of "honorarium,, eamed income
in connection with a bona fide business, trade, or profession, but specifically states tt at
this exemption does not apply when the "sole or piedominant activity of the business,
trade, or profession is making speeches.',
Furthermore, interpreting Section gg50 I (bx I ), Regulatio n lgg32provides :


File No. A-09-097
Page No. 3
"(l) The services are provided in connection with an
individual's business or the individuals practice of or
employment in a bonafide business, trade, or profession,
such as teaching, practicing law, medicine, insurance, real
estate, banking, or building contracting, pursuant to
[Regulations 18932.1 through 1g932.3]; and
"(2) The services are customarily provided in connection
with the business, trade, or profession." (Emphasis added.)
Regulation 18932.1(d) states that "a business whose predominant activity is making
speeches is not 'bona fide' for purposes of [Section s^gsot through g9506].,,
. 
Ifyou give speeches in connection with a "business" from which you receive
income, Regulation 18932.1(d) bars you from receiving these payments because you
would be receiving the payments for providing servicei as a speaker for a business
whose "predominant activity is making speeches.', If no ,.business entity,, is involved,
the payments fall directly within the piohibition of Section 89501 against receiving an
honorarium.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
"(a) 'Honorarium' does not include income eamed from
personal services if:
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, trgal Division
VJ:rb
